verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 subunit beta) is basally a mitochondrial inner membrane protein. However, abundant evidence documents ectopic translocation to the plasma membrane in cancer cells (AML, breast, prostate, lung, liver carcinomas), endothelial cells, and hepatocytes. This translocation occurs via active microtubule-dependent trafficking (DRP1/KIF5B) and mitochondrial membrane fusion to the PM, anchoring the protein on the outer leaflet. The surface pool is functionally active and a documented target for cancer immunotherapy. This represents context-dependent (cancer-cell-state-induced) surface accessibility, not baseline PM residence.
